text.title: go-layout-agentic-workflow Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-layout-agentic-workflow as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: pkg/ reflex: agents add pkg/utils, pkg/helpers from muscle memory without any external consumer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force internal/ first in every prompt. Add the depguard "unused pkg/" check from go-layout-lint.sh to the review stage.

failure-mode.description: Interface in producer package: agents declare repository.UserRepository interface in internal/repository; consumers import the producer, creating fan-in coupling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force consumer-side declaration in the layout-pass prompt: "Interfaces live in the consumer package (service for repo iface)."

failure-mode.description: gin-gonic leakage into service: agents pass *gin.Context past the handler boundary into service methods.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force ctx context.Context in every service and repository signature. The depguard rule "service must not depend on web framework" catches it in CI.

failure-mode.description: Global DB handle: agents create var DB *sql.DB at package scope in internal/repository or internal/config.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force constructor injection from cmd/name/main.go. The review prompt flags global state outside cmd/.

failure-mode.description: Skipped error wrapping: agents return err from repository methods; the service layer has no idea where the error came from.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force %w-wrap on every error crossing a layer boundary. Include this in the code-gen prompt and the review checklist.

failure-mode.description: init() side effects: agents add init() to register handlers or apply migrations, making test-suite startup order brittle.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use explicit constructors called from main. Reject any init() that performs I/O or registration in the review prompt.

failure-mode.description: Context not propagated: agents start goroutines in a service without a derived context, losing cancellation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force errgroup.WithContext or named worker pools with a context parameter in the concurrency pattern prompt.

failure-mode.description: Test on wrong layer: agents test handlers via the real router with a real DB.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force handler tests with mocked services and repository tests with testcontainers-go. Explicitly forbid wiring the full stack in a handler test.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated stdlib APIs: agents invent sql.QueryRowContext signatures that do not exist in the pinned Go version.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the Go version in go.mod and include it in the code-gen prompt context. Require agents to cite the stdlib doc section they are using.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop skipped for layout changes: agents auto-merge PRs that rename a top-level directory or move a package.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Layout churn is high-impact, low-frequency, and irreversible in git history. Require human review for any commit touching cmd/, internal/, or pkg/ root structure.
